prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "直腿弹跳" (straight-leg bounding / pogo run) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person CLEARLY AIRBORNE mid-flight during straight-leg bounding (a forward-traveling stiff-legged drill). The figure is OFF THE GROUND with visible space below both feet. BOTH LEGS ARE LOCKED-OUT STRAIGHT — knees almost completely straightened (only the most minimal bend, NOT the deep knee bend of normal running). The FRONT (left) LEG is straight and swinging forward in front of the body with the foot DORSIFLEXED (toes pulled UP toward the shin) at the top of the swing. The REAR (right) LEG is straight and angled back behind the body with the foot PLANTARFLEXED (toes pointed DOWN). Both arms in vigorous running-style opposition. Torso upright with slight forward lean. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: this is NOT regular running with bent knees — both knees are LOCKED-OUT STRAIGHT throughout the movement; the legs scissor straight-legged through the air.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus) for elastic recoil and hamstring group. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and quadriceps. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "小腿三头肌" pointing to the calf muscles for elastic recoil; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group; "臀大肌 / 股四头肌" pointing to the glute and quadriceps area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
